summary:
The patient likely has acute epiglottitis based on classic signs including high fever, stridor, and dysphagia. Nebulized racemic epinephrine is considered an appropriate empirical intervention pending secure airway establishment because it may reduce swelling temporarily while preparing for more definitive interventions such as endotracheal intubation or cricothyrotomy.
